text.title: AI Incident Response Playbook Examples
text.summary: Worked example: runbook for the "hallucination spike" incident class in a customer-support agent.

example.title: Escalation header (at top)
example.code:
# Hallucination spike — support-agent
> ESCALATION: minute 10 → eng-lead@faion.net (PagerDuty: P12345) > minute 25 → cto@faion.net > Incident channel: #incident-support-agent

example.title: Steps with signal+threshold+action
example.code:
## Step 1 — Confirm signal (budget 2 min) - Signal: `dashboards/support-agent.hallucination_flag_rate` over last 5 min - Threshold: >0.05 - Action: open incident; tag sev-1 if also `user_complaint_rate >0.01` - Post: incident created in tracker
## Step 2 — Throttle (budget 3 min) - Action: `POST /api/v1/cli/throttle?feature=support-agent&qps=10` - Post: `metrics/support-agent.qps` <= 10
## Step 3 — Decide kill-switch vs rollback (budget 5 min) - Signal: time since last good eval result - If <24h → flip kill-switch (Step 4) - Else → rollback to prior bundle (Step 5)
## Step 4 — Kill-switch (budget 2 min) - Action: `POST /api/v1/cli/kill-switch?feature=support-agent` - Post: `feature_flag.support-agent.enabled = false` - Fallback: rule-based responder activates automatically
## Step 5 — Rollback to prior bundle (budget 5 min) - Action: use rollback-button (see [[agent-rollback-button-design]]) - Post: bundle_version matches prior stable
## Step 6 — Notify customers (budget 10 min) - Action: send status-page update; email impacted accounts (list from `users_affected.csv`)
## Step 7 — Open postmortem (within 24h) - Use [[ai-incident-postmortem-template]]

example.title: Counter-example: vague step
example.code: Step 2: Check if things look weird and decide what to do
example.note: No signal, no threshold, no action, no budget. Useless under stress.
